name: MikroTik
description: MikroTik (officially SIA "Mikrotīkls") is a Latvian networking equipment manufacturer founded in 1996 that specializes in high-performance, cost-effective routers, switches, and wireless systems. The company is widely recognized for its RouterOS software, which provides advanced enterprise-level features—such as BGP, OSPF, MPLS, and complex firewalls—at a fraction of the cost of traditional enterprise vendors.

name: Ubiquiti
description: Ubiquiti Inc. is an American technology company known for manufacturing high-performance, license-free networking and physical security products. Their ecosystem is highly regarded by both tech enthusiasts ("prosumers") and enterprises for offering high-end features—like centralized cloud management and advanced security—without the ongoing subscription fees typical of other enterprise brands

description: ALFA Network Inc. is a prominent Taiwanese manufacturer specializing in high-performance wireless networking equipment. name: Mimosa
description: Mimosa is redefining how people worldwide gain exceptional internet access through innovative wireless technology. name: RF elements
description: RF elements is a European manufacturer specializing in high-performance antenna technology for Wireless Internet Service Providers (WISPs) and fixed wireless networks. They are best known for their Horn Antennas and the proprietary TwistPort™ connector system, designed to solve signal interference and RF noise in dense wireless environments

name: 60GHz Wireless
description: MikroTik's 60GHz Wireless Category consists of high-speed, fiber-like wireless solutions designed to replace cables or provide high-bandwidth links over short to medium distances. These devices operate in the unlicensed 60GHz spectrum, which avoids the interference common in 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands.

name: 60GHz antennas
description: All MikroTik 60GHz devices are integrated units, meaning the radio and antenna are housed together in a single enclosure. There are no standalone 'antenna-only' products for the 60GHz spectrum in their catalog, as the high-frequency nature of 60GHz requires integrated beamforming arrays to manage signal precision.

name: Wireless Wire
description: The Wireless Wire category consists of pre-configured, 'plug-and-play' kits designed to replace a physical Gigabit Ethernet cable with a high-speed 60GHz wireless link. These kits include two pre-paired devices that automatically form a secure, AES-encrypted bridge once powered on and pointed at each other.

name: Ethernet Routers
description: MikroTik offers a wide range of Ethernet routers running the powerful RouterOS operating system, catering to everything from compact home setups to massive carrier-grade infrastructure. Their hardware is known for providing professional-grade features—like advanced firewalls, VPN support, and deep packet management—at a much lower price point than competitors

name: Cloud Core Router
description: MikroTik’s Cloud Core Router (CCR) category is their flagship lineup of carrier-grade routers designed for high-performance tasks like core routing, BGP peering, and heavy firewalling. Unlike the consumer-focused hAP or business-grade RB series, CCRs are built for massive data throughput, often reaching wire-speed performance on all ports simultaneously.

name: PowerBox
description: The MikroTik PowerBox category is a specialized line of outdoor routers designed primarily for powering other devices via PoE (Power over Ethernet). These devices act as compact outdoor hubs, allowing you to mount one unit on a pole or tower and use it to power and manage several other devices (like wireless APs or IP cameras) without running multiple power cables.

name: RouterBoards
description: RouterBoard (RB) is the foundation of MikroTik's hardware lineup. While 'RouterBoard' originally referred to the physical circuit boards sold for DIY assembly, it now encompasses almost all MikroTik devices that run RouterOS. If the CCR series is the 'heavy-duty engine,' the RouterBoard series is the 'versatile chassis' that ranges from tiny home labs to rugged industrial controllers.
